title: Kamala Narayana Temple, Degaon
tagline: Kamala Narayana Temple, Degaon — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Kamala Narayana Temple is at Degaon in Belgaum District, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Kamala Narayana Temple is at Degaon in Belgaum District, Karnataka, India. The temple was built by the Kadamba dynasty. Kamal Narayan Temple was constructed by Tippoja, the chief architect of Kamala Devi, the Queen of the Kadamba king Sivachitta Permadi in the 12th century. This temple built in 1174.

topPlaces.name: Kittur
topPlaces.description: Kittur or Kitturu, historically known as Kittoor, is a town and a taluk in the Belagavi district of the Indian state of Karnataka. It was part of Bailhongal taluka but was declared as an independent taluka on 23 October 2012 by the Chief Minister of Karnataka on the inauguration of Kittur Utsav.

topPlaces.description: Kittur is a fort located in the Karnataka state of India, it is the former capital of a minor principality as well as a major archaeological site.

items.note: Degaon is a village in Belgaum district in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is famous for Kadamba style Kamala Narayana Temple.

Kittur or Kitturu, historically known as Kittoor, is a town and a taluk in the Belagavi district of the Indian state of Karnataka. It was part of Bailhongal taluka but was declared as an independent taluka on 23 October 2012 by the Chief Minister of Karnataka on the inauguration of Kittur Utsav.

Kittur is a fort located in the Karnataka state of India, it is the former capital of a minor principality as well as a major archaeological site.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kamala Narayana Temple, Degaon?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kamala Narayana Temple, Degaon is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kamala Narayana Temple, Degaon?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Belagavi Airport (35 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (48 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kamala Narayana Temple, Degaon?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kamala Narayana Temple, Degaon at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kamala Narayana Temple, Degaon?
faq.a: Stays in Kamala Narayana Temple, Degaon start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kamala Narayana Temple, Degaon?
faq.a: The top attractions are Degaon, Basarkhod, Degulhalli, Kittur, Tigadolli.
